_comment: Generic MCP server config for browser-bridge. Most clients (Claude Code's .mcp.json, Claude Desktop, and others) use this `mcpServers` shape — copy the `browser-bridge` entry into your client's config. Point `command` at the installed binary: ~/.browser-bridge/browser-bridge on macOS, ~/.local/share/browser-bridge/browser-bridge on Linux, or /mnt/c/Users/YOU/AppData/Local/browser-bridge/browser-bridge.exe from WSL when using Windows Chrome. Use an ABSOLUTE path (most clients do not expand ~), and it does not depend on your shell PATH. The server speaks MCP over stdio with no arguments. Codex uses TOML instead of JSON — see the README for that snippet.
